message:
feat(doctor): expand to full environment health checker with --fix and --only

Replace the extension-only doctor command with a comprehensive environment
health checker that inspects configuration, directories, authentication,
shell tools, and sessions in addition to extension preflight analysis.

The path argument is now optional — omitting it runs all environment checks.
Two new flags control behavior:
  --fix    automatically repairs safe issues (missing directories, permissions)
  --only   runs specific check categories (config,dirs,auth,shell,sessions,extensions)

New src/doctor.rs module (1223 lines) implements categorized checks with
severity levels (Pass/Info/Warn/Fail), fixability annotations, and three
output formats (text, json, markdown). The module uses a callback-driven
architecture where each category registers its findings independently,
allowing --only filtering without conditional compilation.

Supporting changes:
- auth.rs: add provider_names() to enumerate stored credential providers
  (used by doctor auth checks)
- session_index.rs: expose build_meta_from_file and walk_sessions as
  pub(crate) for doctor session health scanning
- cli.rs: Doctor path is now Option<String>, add --fix (bool) and
  --only (Option<String>) arguments
- main.rs: refactored handle_doctor delegates to doctor::run_doctor,
  parses --only into HashSet<CheckCategory> with validation, exits
  with code 1 on Severity::Fail for CI integration

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -814,16 +814,22 @@ pub enum Commands {
     /// Open configuration UI
     Config,
 
-    /// Diagnose extension compatibility and suggest fixes
+    /// Diagnose environment health and extension compatibility
     Doctor {
-        /// Extension path or directory to check
-        path: String,
+        /// Extension path to check (omit to run all environment checks)
+        path: Option<String>,
         /// Output format: text (default), json, markdown
         #[arg(long, default_value = "text")]
         format: String,
         /// Extension policy profile to check against
         #[arg(long)]
         policy: Option<String>,
+        /// Automatically fix safe issues (missing dirs, permissions)
+        #[arg(long)]
+        fix: bool,
+        /// Run specific categories: config,dirs,auth,shell,sessions,extensions
+        #[arg(long)]
+        only: Option<String>,
     },
 }
